title: poa flagellifera
intro: Poa flagellifera, also known as the tussock grass or flagellate poa, is a type of perennial grass native to New Zealand and Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Poa flagellifera include relief from colds, fever, and coughs, as well as the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Poa flagellifera typically involve making teas or infusions from the plant's leaves and flowers, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications.
